title: Tephroseris cantabrica
taxonomy: Tephroseris cantabrica belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a species of flowering plant within the genus Tephroseris. Tephroseris cantabrica is a rare alpine plant native to the Cantabrian Mountains in northern Spain. Its classification also places it within the tribe Senecioneae, a group of daisy-like plants. The plant is further categorized under the subfamily Asteroideae within the Asteraceae family.
uses: Tephroseris cantabrica has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots and leaves have been employed to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that Tephroseris cantabrica may have antimicrobial properties, which could contribute to its traditional medicinal uses. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes. Further investigation is required to fully understand the potential benefits and risks associated with Tephroseris cantabrica as a medicinal plant.

properties: Tephroseris cantabrica, also known as the Cantabrian rockrose, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Additionally, Tephroseris cantabrica has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory issues. preparations: Tephroseris cantabrica is a plant species that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the Iberian Peninsula.
